stories.title: First Date Chaos
stories.template: It was a {adjective1} night when I brought a {noun1} to the restaurant and started {verbIng1}.

stories.title: Gym Advice
stories.template: My trainer says if I pour {liquid1} directly onto my {bodyPart1} for {number1} minutes a day, I will become unbelievably {adjective1}.

stories.title: Haunted Rental
stories.template: At 3am we heard a {sound1} coming from the {adjective1} {room1}, followed by at least twelve furious {pluralAnimal1}.

stories.title: Wedding Speech
stories.template: During the wedding speech, my {relationship1} became extremely {emotion1} and accidentally {verbPast1} a full {noun1} into the cake.

stories.title: IKEA Trip
stories.template: We went in for {{intent}}. We left with that, plus {{extra}}, plus an unresolved argument about {{argument}} that started somewhere near the bedroom displays. The fight only ended in the cafeteria when one of us silently bought the other {{snack}}. We made it to the car, looked at each other, and {{exit}}.

stories.title: Thermostat War
stories.template: I keep the thermostat at {{temp1}}. They insist on {{temp2}}, which is criminal. Every time I leave the room, they {{tactic}}. After three weeks of cold war diplomacy, we finally agreed: {{verdict}}. Neither of us is honoring it.

stories.title: DoorDash Debate
stories.template: {{minutes}} into the DoorDash debate, neither of us is closer to ordering. They want {{option1}}. I want {{option2}}. We are both hungry enough to be passive-aggressive about it. The compromise — {{compromise}} — is somehow worse than both of our original picks, and we order it anyway.

stories.title: The Dishes Conversation
stories.template: We are once again standing in the kitchen having The Dishes Conversation. Specifically about {{dish}}, which has been in the sink since Tuesday. They open with {{tone}}. I respond with my usual defense: {{yourExcuse}}. They counter with {{theirCounter}}, which is, annoyingly, accurate. The conversation resolves the way it always does: {{resolution}}.

stories.title: Shared Streaming Account
stories.template: We were watching {{show}} together. We agreed: no episodes alone. I opened the app tonight and the algorithm casually informed me they were {{episodes}} episodes ahead. The evidence was right there: {{evidence}}. When confronted, they offered: {{apology}}. I have already started watching it twice as fast just to catch up out of spite.

stories.title: Moving Day
stories.template: We packed up {{fromPlace}} into 47 boxes, most of them labeled simply {{boxLabel}}. We moved into {{toPlace}} and immediately discovered that {{broken}} did not survive the trip. {{missingItem}} has vanished entirely; it is in one of the boxes, but which one is a problem for future us. We sat on the kitchen floor with {{delivery}}, and at 11 PM we had a low-energy argument about {{argument}} that we both knew wasn't really about that.

stories.title: Meeting the Parents
stories.template: I wore {{outfit}}, which was either perfect or a disaster. I brought {{gift}}. {{parentName}} hugged me. Dinner was {{dish}}, which I claimed to love. Somewhere between the main and dessert, {{parentName}} asked {{question}}. I panicked and answered {{answer}}. Then, with no warning, they told the story about {{story}}. On the car ride home, the verdict came in: {{verdict}}. Honestly? I'll take it.

stories.title: Couples Yoga
stories.template: We signed up for couples yoga at {{studio}}. The instructor was {{instructor}}. We got through warm-up. Then she announced {{pose}}, and within thirty seconds my partner made {{noise}} loud enough for the whole room. I leaned over and whispered {{whisper}}. The couple next to us was {{neighbor}} the entire time, somehow perfectly. In the car afterward, one of us finally said {{aftermath}}. We are not going back.

stories.title: Anniversary Dinner
stories.template: For our anniversary we booked the kind of restaurant {{restaurant}} requires {{dressCode}}. We ordered {{drink}} and {{appetizer}}, which arrived looking like art. Right as the candle flickered between us, {{interruption}} ruined the moment completely. They handed me a small box. Inside was {{gift}}. My reaction was {{reaction}}, which was not the reaction they were hoping for. The night ended the only way it could: {{ending}}.

stories.title: Couples Therapy, First Session
stories.template: Our therapist's name is {{therapist}}. She started with: {{question}}. I gave the answer I had clearly rehearsed: {{yourAnswer}}. Then my partner answered: {{theirAnswer}}. I was not ready for that. {{tissue}} reached for the tissues first. Before we left, she gave us homework: {{homework}}. On the car ride home, {{carRide}}. As soon as I got inside I texted the group chat: {{verdict}}. We have booked next week.

stories.title: Road Trip Fight
stories.template: We were six hours into a drive to {{destination}}. Specifically, hour {{hour}}. The fight started over {{trigger}}, which was the smallest possible thing. Within minutes, the {{snack}} between us had become weaponized evidence of an unrelated betrayal. {{playlist}} played, very quietly, for {{silence}} minutes of dead silence. Peace was eventually negotiated at a gas station, brokered entirely by a single purchase: {{peace}}. We did not speak about it again.

stories.title: Wedding Planning Meltdown
stories.template: We were going to get married at {{venue}}. Budget: {{budget}}. Real budget: {{newBudget}}. They insisted on inviting {{guestThey}}. I am still quietly trying to uninvite {{guestYou}}. The wedding colors were {{color1}}, then somehow also {{color2}}, then officially neither. {{vendor}} ghosted us in week three. The dinner menu fight was, of all things, about {{menuFight}}. The registry includes {{registry}}, which someone is going to buy us, and we will have to thank them for it. The wedding was saved entirely by {{compromise}}. At 2 AM the night before the final headcount was due, one of us said: {{verdict}}. We are still getting married. Probably.

stories.title: Destination Wedding Trip
stories.template: We flew to {{location}} for {{couple}}'s wedding. {{flightProblem}} happened immediately. The rental was supposed to be perfect, except {{rental}}. By day two, I deeply regretted packing {{outfit}}. At the rehearsal dinner, {{rehearsalDinner}}. The best man's speech was about {{speech}} and lasted nineteen minutes. The signature cocktail was {{drink}}, which we ordered eight of. On the dance floor, {{danceFloor}}. Throughout the entire trip, we were quietly keeping a secret from the rest of the group: {{secret}}. At the airport, waiting at the gate, exhausted and sunburned, one of us whispered: {{verdict}}. Worth it.
